Title: Freeze the 50-document packet register
Objective: Start the compiler from a dated, editable register instead of a loose list of candidate records.
Action: Download the seeded 50-document packet register, save a dated working copy, and assign initial disposition, source-copy status, duplicate status, and boundary ruling for every DOC50 row.
Proof Gate: All 50 packet rows have a working disposition and named next action before any row receives a final FRUS number.
Source Note: Compiler docket item generated from the seeded source-copy packet register and the 50-document candidate register.

Title: Submit the RG 59 OWI 103.9166 source-copy batch
Objective: Turn the strongest OWI purport-card hits into actual Central Decimal File source copies.
Proof Gate: Each pulled file has date, author/post, recipient, full text, enclosures, routing marks, FRUS duplicate proof, and a promote/hold/boundary ruling.
Links: Pull sheet: #pull-sheets; Purport cards: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/88176221; Central Decimal Files: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/302021
Source Note: First docket pull from RG 59 Purport Lists and Cards, NAID 88176221, M973 roll 607 images 0500-0750.

Title: Submit the RG 59 CIAA/OIAA 103.9161 source-copy batch
Objective: Recover State/CIAA cables for Spanish-language shortwave, WNBI reception, motion pictures, and hemispheric information service work.
Proof Gate: Each Latin America candidate has a source copy, post or office routing, audience/reception evidence, and cross-link to RG 84 or RG 229 where available.
Links: Pull sheet: #pull-sheets; Purport cards: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/88176221; 103.9161 catalog search: https://catalog.archives.gov/search?q=103.9161&recordGroupNumber=59
Source Note: First docket pull from RG 59 Purport Lists and Cards, NAID 88176221, M973 roll 607 images 0095-0125.

Title: Pull the RG 208 long-range policy directive batch
Objective: Anchor the wartime and liberation chronology in policy guidance before choosing implementation and reaction documents.
Action: Request RG 208 Record Set of Policy Directives source copies for Germany, France, Austria, Poland, Czechoslovakia, Switzerland, Netherlands, and Thailand directive candidates.
Proof Gate: Directive packet records issue date, target country, themes, approving office, relation to State/handoff, and duplicate-check result.
Links: Pull sheet: #pull-sheets; Record Set of Policy Directives: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/648565; Germany directive: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/4732380
Source Note: Directive docket item generated from RG 208 Record Set of Policy Directives candidate records.

Title: Run the FRUS duplicate gate for every promoted row
Objective: Keep the retro volume from duplicating already-published FRUS documents and preserve the audit trail for each selection decision.
Action: For every promoted DOC50 row, save an exact-title or exact-file-number History.state.gov search and a broader topical search with checked date and result count.
Proof Gate: The packet register records search URL, checked date, result count, duplicate status, and the final duplicate ruling for each promoted candidate.
Source Note: Quality gate generated from the 50-document candidate register and public FRUS exact-match checks.

Title: Pair first pulls with RG 84 post-file reporting
Objective: Give selected documents diplomatic context by pairing Washington guidance with embassy implementation, clearance, or foreign reaction.
Action: Search priority RG 84 post files for London, Lisbon, Madrid, Stockholm, Bern, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, and Cuba using OWI, USIS, OIAA, CIAA, radio, films, press, propaganda, censorship, WNBI, and shortwave terms.
Proof Gate: At least one post-file implementation or reaction record is linked to each promoted policy/output cluster where RG 84 coverage exists.
Links: State cable leads: #state-cables; London RG 84: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/1667864; Mexico RG 84: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/1677124
Source Note: Post-file docket item generated from RG 84 State cable leads and the first pull sheets.

Title: Pull the FDR Library OWI White House decision packet
Objective: Prevent the volume from leaning only on agency records by checking presidential routing, oversight, and public-line control.
Action: Pull the President's Secretary's File OWI subject/confidential files and Survey of Intelligence folders for source copies, routing, FDR action, and relation to State/OWI decisions.
Proof Gate: White House packet identifies which FDR Library records promote, support, or merely contextualize the document chronology.
Links: Pull sheet: #pull-sheets; OWI subject file: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/16620603; OWI confidential file: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/16609748
Source Note: White House docket item generated from FDR Library President's Secretary's File candidates.

Title: Build source-copy packets before assigning final chronology numbers
Objective: Make every selected candidate defensible as a FRUS document, not just an interesting source lead.
Action: For each promoted candidate, complete a packet with repository, series, box/file or NAID, date, author, recipient, classification, full source copy or image link, enclosures, duplicate proof, selection rationale, boundary ruling, and cross-references.
Proof Gate: No row receives final chronology placement until the packet standard is complete and the promotion rationale is explicit.
Links: Packet template CSV: templates/source-copy-packet-template.csv; Packet template Markdown: templates/source-copy-packet-template.md; Compiler guide: compiler-guide.html
Source Note: Packet-build docket item generated from the compiler guide, packet template, selection rules, and pull sheets.
